corpus: .claude/harvest-findings/raw_conversation/ (3 transcripts; only the 1738 transcript's 7 stamps were unharvested — the 1223 and 1502 transcripts were already processed in prior raw_knowledge runs)

findings.claim: At host scope (state = NULL) the consumer-clear identity paste0(sid,"#",dp,"#",bn) is doubly blind — dp is "" for any env shortcut (no upload datapath) and bn is "" because there is no state$bound_names to read — so the shortcut's typed value sc is the ONLY rename-sensitive signal available there.
findings.detail: This is why an env→env shortcut rename (no file at either end) leaves the identity constant and the ADR 0025 §3b new-source clear never fires; the ADR 0026 fix appends sc to the identity gated on is.null(state).
findings.verification: Read consumer_upstream_source_state (R/paintr-server.R:2582-2648). dp is "" unless fv$datapath present (2600-2602) -> empty for env shortcut. bn reads state$bound_names[[sid]] (2604), which is NULL at host scope (state=NULL) -> "" (2621). The id_str branch on is.null(state) appends sc only at host scope: paste0(sid,"#",dp,"#",bn,"#",sc) (2637-2638). So at host scope sc is the only rename-sensitive signal, exactly as claimed. Cited range 2575-2640 drifted slightly to 2582-2648.

findings.claim: The ADR 0025 §3b boot-seed contracts are enforced by src_seen and user_supplied, NOT by the identity string, so adding a component to the identity cannot regress them.
findings.detail: changed_since_last = src_seen && !identical(identity,last_identity) && isTRUE(user_supplied) — first render has src_seen=FALSE so a boot/spec seed never clears regardless of identity; and user_supplied requires nzchar(sc) && !identical(sc,def), so a shortcut equal to its default at boot is not new work. This refuted the ADR's worry that sc had been deliberately excluded for a §3b-safety reason.
findings.verification: Read consumer_clear_for_new_source (R/paintr-server.R:2672-2678): changed_since_last = src_seen && !identical(identity,last_identity) && isTRUE(user_supplied) (2674-2675); first_render_on_upload = !src_seen && is.null(seed) && isTRUE(uploaded) (2676). First render has src_seen=FALSE so changed_since_last cannot fire regardless of identity. user_supplied is computed in consumer_upstream_source_state as nzchar(dp) || (nzchar(sc) && !identical(sc,def)) (2644). So the §3b contracts ride on src_seen and user_supplied, not the identity string -- adding sc to identity cannot regress them. Claim holds. Cited 2637 (user_supplied) -> now 2644; 2665-2671 (clear fn) -> 2672-2678.

findings.claim: The host-scope cleared_for_identity/held_clear latch exists only to survive the trailing double-render an UPLOAD fires; an env→env shortcut rename is a single reactive beat, so the base clear trips once and a subsequent legitimate re-pick is not re-clobbered.
findings.detail: Verified empirically by an explicit re-pick-after-clear-survives e2e assertion (set shared_col to a column of the new frame, wait_for_idle, assert get_value equals it) — green under the fix. So the latch does not need extension for the env-rename path.
findings.verification: Read the host-scope held_clear block in ptr_bind_shared_consumer_uis (R/paintr-server.R:3318-3334), gated on is.null(state). held_clear re-asserts the clear on the one render after a base clear edge while current still carries the stale pick (3321-3323); cleared_for_identity is armed on a clear edge then consumed/released next render (3326-3330). The comment (3309-3317) states the purpose is the upload's trailing double-render; a single-beat env rename trips once and a later re-pick is not re-clobbered. test-adr26-host-env-rename-clear.R has the explicit re-pick-survives assertion (line 69-71: get_value(shared_col)=='trt', label 'host latch does not re-clobber'). Matches claim. Cited line 3314 -> block at 3318-3334.

findings.claim: The layer_name fallback added 2026-05-29 to the bn derivation reads state$bound_names[[layer_name]], which is ALSO NULL at host scope, so it does nothing for the host env-rename case despite looking like a fix for the same bug class.
findings.detail: That fallback fixes the bare-data-layer source-id-vs-layer-name key duality (ADR 0025 contract ii) at single-instance scope where state is real; at host scope its guard layer_name %in% names(state$bound_names) is FALSE because names(NULL) is NULL, so bn stays "".
findings.verification: Read the bn derivation in consumer_upstream_source_state (R/paintr-server.R:2603-2622). The layer_name fallback: if (is.null(r) && !is.null(layer_name) && layer_name %in% names(state$bound_names)) r <- state$bound_names[[layer_name]] (2617-2620). At host scope state=NULL so names(state$bound_names) is NULL, the guard is FALSE, and bn stays "" -- the fallback does nothing for the host env-rename case, exactly as claimed. Cited 2596-2615 drifted to ~2603-2622.

findings.claim: ADR 0026 was fixed with candidate #2 (append sc to the identity) rather than #1 (host-aware bound name), gated on is.null(state) so the single-instance identity string stays byte-identical and that verified path is provably untouched.
findings.detail: Fix #1 (thread a bound_name_for callback computing panel_owned_binding_name) was the ADR's plan-of-record but duplicates binding-name logic the host snap block already computes (drift risk); #2 is one line and provably single-instance-safe by construction. Single-instance runs with state != NULL → the else-branch keeps paste0(sid,"#",dp,"#",bn).
findings.verification: Read id_str (R/paintr-server.R:2637-2641): if (is.null(state)) paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn,'#',sc) else paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn). This is candidate #2 (append sc), gated on is.null(state); the single-instance else-branch keeps the original byte-identical paste0(sid,'#',dp,'#',bn). The comment (2628-2636) documents this is ADR 0026's chosen fix over the host-aware bound name (#1). dev/adr/0026-fix-host-env-rename-never-clears.html exists. Matches claim. Cited 2630-2635 -> 2637-2641.

findings.claim: test-spec-seed-derived-column-browser.R (the NON-shared spec-seed derived-column e2e) is a confirmed parallel-only boot-tail flake — it fails under TESTTHAT_PARALLEL CPU contention (derived column 'adj' not in y picker choices at boot) but passes green serially.
findings.detail: Observed FAIL 2 (lines 38 and 45 labels) in the parallel full gate, then FAIL 0/PASS 11 re-running the spec-seed-derived-column cluster serially. Structural corroboration it was not the ADR-0026 regression: this file is the non-shared path (state != NULL) where the host-gated sc change is byte-identical. Sibling of the already-harvested parallel-boottail-flake-shared-spec-seed-derived (the SHARED variant) and parallel-gate-flakes-seeding-cluster.
findings.verification: Re-verified @ HEAD: trigger was the unrelated CLAUDE.md /stamp-pointer removal; the parallel-flake-green-serial claim is unchanged and reconfirmed this session. Claim holds.

findings.claim: Reproducing the host env→env rename clear requires a fixture with TWO env frames sharing an overlapping column (body_mass_g) plus a unique marker per frame; the adjacent adr25-host-shared-source-reset fixture has only one env frame and so cannot express the rename.
findings.detail: Overlapping column is the load-bearing proxy-trap guard (selectInput silently drops a selection unique to the old frame → false green); the unique markers (grp / trt) let the test assert each frame actually loaded so the clear assertion is not vacuously reading a still-frame-A picker. The red test failed on HEAD with get_value == "body_mass_g" (the stale ride).
findings.verification: Both files exist (committed in aee7fd3). Fixture defines chickish {grp, body_mass_g} and plantish {trt, body_mass_g} — overlapping body_mass_g + unique grp/trt markers. The red run on the pre-fix tree failed at the clear assertion with got: "body_mass_g".
